ticket.title: Design v1 AI staff system: 丞相-only agent named Mica (ticketing + handoff prompts)
ticket.objective: Define the first shippable version of an AI staff system with a single Chief-of-Staff agent (丞相) named “Mica” that converts messy requests into structured, reviewable task tickets and prepares handoff prompts, borrowing Edict-style governance but keeping it simple.
ticket.context: Billy is a PhD student working across Unity tactical/auto-battler game dev, academic research on LLM-based repair/agents, academic writing, and lightweight daily ops. Billy prefers structured outputs, practical implementation, and human review before important decisions. This ticket is for the general project and should establish the initial governance + operating format for Mica (丞相) only, without implementing other staff roles yet.
assumptions: “Edict governance style” means: clear role boundaries, explicit decision authority staying with Billy, standardized reviewable artifacts, and lightweight process checks (e.g., required fields, risks, next action).
Mica will run as a system prompt/persona within an LLM interface (e.g., ChatGPT/API) rather than as a fully autonomous application at v1.
The output format should be a strict schema (e.g., Pydantic/JSON) to make tickets machine-parseable and consistent.
Handoff prompts should target future specialist agents (e.g., Game Engineer, Research Scout) but those agents are not implemented in v1; Mica only prepares the prompt text.
missing_information: Where will Mica be used first: ChatGPT custom GPT, API-backed internal tool, or other? (Affects formatting constraints, memory handling, and storage.)
Do you want tickets stored anywhere (GitHub issues/Notion/markdown files), or is chat-only acceptable for v1?
What parts of “Edict” are must-have vs. avoid? (e.g., strict checklists, approval gates, confidence scoring, audit logs.)
Preferred ticket schema fields beyond the current baseline (title/objective/context/assumptions/risks/next action). Any required tags, estimates, deadlines, or acceptance criteria?
Should Mica ask clarifying questions by default, or only when blocked? (Current preference implied: only when necessary.)
ticket.next_action: Draft the v1 governance + specification document for Mica: (1) role definition and non-goals, (2) ticket schema (Pydantic/JSON), (3) operating principles and escalation rules, (4) example transformations (messy → ticket) across Billy’s domains, and (5) handoff prompt template + routing rules; then present to Billy for approval.
risks: Over-specifying governance early could slow usage; under-specifying could lead to inconsistent tickets and extra back-and-forth.
If the schema is too rigid, it may not fit mixed-domain tasks; if too loose, it won’t be reviewable/parseable.
Ambiguity around storage/integration could cause rework when moving from chat-only to a tool (e.g., GitHub/Notion).
Handoff prompts may be premature without well-defined specialist capabilities; mitigate by keeping them templated and optional.
ticket.deliverable_format: A short v1 spec (1–2 pages) plus a strict ticket JSON/Pydantic schema and 3–5 worked examples; include a reusable handoff prompt template and routing table.
ticket.handoff_prompt: You are a Writing Editor helping define a minimal governance/spec for an AI Chief-of-Staff agent named Mica (丞相). Produce a concise v1 spec that: (a) states responsibilities/non-goals and Billy’s decision authority; (b) defines a strict ticket schema (JSON/Pydantic) for converting messy requests into structured tickets; (c) defines when to ask clarifying questions vs. proceed with assumptions; (d) includes lightweight Edict-inspired governance (quality bar, review questions, risks, next action); (e) provides 3–5 examples across game dev, academic research, writing, and daily ops; (f) provides a handoff prompt template for future specialist agents and a simple routing table. Keep it practical and not over-engineered.
ticket.archive_notes: If approved, save: Mica’s role definition, ticket schema, routing rules, and the standard handoff-prompt template as canonical governance for the AI staff system v1.

review_questions: What platform will host Mica in v1 (chat-only vs. integrated tool)?
Do you want ticket outputs to be strictly JSON every time, or is “JSON + brief markdown” acceptable?
What are the 2–3 non-negotiable Edict-style governance elements you want included?
Should Mica proactively propose specialists and handoff prompts every time, or only when the task clearly matches a domain?
Any required fields to add now (e.g., effort estimate, deadline, links, acceptance criteria)?
suggested_user_reply: Mica will be used in chat-only for v1, outputs should be strict JSON, and the Edict elements I want are: clear decision authority, explicit assumptions/risks, and a required next action + review questions. No storage integration yet; keep the schema minimal (no estimates/deadlines for now).
